tools.name: get_consumer_lag
tools.description: Read the last-emitted Kafka consumer lag for one of the platform's consumer groups. Values come from the metrics loop's Redis cache — no live Kafka query. Returns null when the cache is empty.

inputSchema.description:
No arguments today. The `consumer_group` field is a forward hook
— once we expose more groups than `worker-dispatcher` (Wave 2), the
agent will pass a specific group name. Defaulting to
`worker-dispatcher` preserves the read shape until then.

consumer_group.description: Kafka consumer group to inspect. Only 'worker-dispatcher' is exposed today.
consumer_group.title: Consumer Group

inputSchema.title: GetConsumerLagInput

tools.name: get_dag_state
tools.description: Fetch the immediate dependency graph around one job — direct parents and direct children with statuses. Use to answer 'why is this job waiting?' or 'what will unblock if I replay?'

job_id.description: Seed job. Its parents (dependencies) and children (dependents) are traversed one hop each direction.
job_id.title: Job Id

tools.name: get_deploy_history
tools.description: Return what version + revision is running right now (a single 'current' entry). Deploy history over time is a planned follow-up that needs a `deploy_markers` table. Today the agent gets the source of truth for what's live.

tools.name: get_incident
tools.description: Fetch full details for one incident by id, including the `extra_data` blob the emitting source attached.

tools.name: get_postgres_health
tools.description: Ping the primary database and read a small set of activity stats. Falls back cleanly on non-Postgres backends (SQLite in tests) — the `dialect` field tells the agent what it looked at.

tools.name: get_redis_health
tools.description: Ping Redis and read a short list of stats from INFO. Never raises — an `ok=false` result *is* the interesting signal.

tools.name: get_trace
tools.description: Fetch every artifact carrying a given trace_id — job rows plus audit-log rows if `include_audit=true`. Useful for building a cold-start context around one incident: the trace is the correlation key that stitches the browser → API → worker → DB path.

include_audit.description: Include audit rows sharing this trace_id.
include_audit.title: Include Audit

tools.name: invalidate_cache_key
tools.description: Delete one Redis key (allowlisted prefixes only). Use to force refresh of a stale cache after fixing the underlying data. Idempotent — a follow-up call finds nothing to delete and returns `deleted=false`.

key.description: Exact Redis key to delete. Must start with one of ['cache:', 'jobs:cache:', 'kafka:consumer_lag:', 'read_model:'].
key.title: Key

tools.name: list_active_alerts
tools.description: List unresolved alerts scoped to the caller's tenant. Alerts come from platform sources (SLO breach, DLQ threshold, chaos runs, etc.). Prefer receiving the HMAC-signed webhook if you can — this endpoint is a poll fallback.

severity.description: Filter by severity: `info`, `warning`, `critical`. Omit for all.
severity.title: Severity

tools.name: list_audit_events
tools.description: Read the immutable audit log for the caller's tenant. Every user + agent + chaos action lands here — use it to reconcile state after a restart or to check what an operator did before the current incident. Filter by `action`, `action_prefix` (e.g. `agent.`), or `principal_type` (`user` vs `service_account`).

action_prefix.description: Prefix match against the action string. Useful for whole streams — `agent.` covers all agent activity, `chaos.` covers chaos, `service_account.` covers principal lifecycle. Ignored when `action` is set.
action_prefix.title: Action Prefix

principal_type.description: Filter to `user` (human) or `service_account` (machine) actors. Omit for both.
principal_type.title: Principal Type

tools.name: list_dlq_messages
tools.description: List jobs currently in the dead-letter state, most recent first. Includes the LLM triage row for each job when the platform has one, so the agent can build on the first-pass analysis instead of starting from raw error strings.

tools.name: list_incidents
tools.description: List incidents (alerts) scoped to the caller's tenant. Defaults to unresolved; pass `include_resolved=true` to see history. Superset of `list_active_alerts` — that tool is the cheap poll path when you know you want unresolved only.

include_resolved.description: When true, include incidents that have been resolved. Useful for reviewing history; the default matches how `list_active_alerts` behaves.
include_resolved.title: Include Resolved

tools.name: pause_dag
tools.description: Pause promotion of WAITING children in the DAG rooted at `root_job_id`. The DependencyResolver consumer checks the pause key before promoting; while set, no child promotes. Effect self-cleans on TTL (default 10 minutes). Idempotent.

root_job_id.description: Root of the DAG to pause. Any WAITING child whose chain of parents reaches this root won't be promoted while the flag is set.
root_job_id.title: Root Job Id

ttl_seconds.description: How long the pause is active. Default 10 minutes.
ttl_seconds.title: Ttl Seconds

tools.name: replay_dlq_messages
tools.description: Replay dead-lettered jobs through the existing job-submission path — resets retry counts and re-publishes to job.submitted. Uses the same code path as the admin Replay button. Idempotent.

job_type.description: Restrict replay to one job type. Omit to include every dead-lettered job in the tenant.
job_type.title: Job Type

limit.description: Maximum number of jobs to replay in one call. Applied after the DB fetch.
limit.title: Limit

tools.name: restart_consumer_group
tools.description: Clear the chaos kill flag on a Kafka consumer group so its supervisor will restart it. Paired with `kill_consumer`; use this to end a chaos run cleanly. Idempotent — repeat calls with the same idempotency_key are safe.

idempotency_key.description: Caller-supplied token. Repeat calls with the same value + same arguments return the same result without re-executing.
idempotency_key.title: Idempotency Key

tools.name: search_traces
tools.description: Scan recent jobs by status / type / recency and return the matching trace_ids so the agent can drill into each with `get_trace`. Cheap — hits the jobs table with the usual indexes.

since_hours.description: Only include jobs whose `created_at` is within the last N hours. Omit for no time bound.
since_hours.title: Since Hours
